$schema-note: One entry per generated/imported asset. commercial_ok gates the Phase 8 launch audit.

assets.source: tripo-starter
assets.license: Tripo Starter (paid) — verify commercial ownership terms before shipping

generation.prompt: A low-poly stylized game monster: a small quadruped goblin-slime creature with stubby legs, big glowing eyes, spiky back, and clawed feet. Clean game-ready topology, T-pose, neutral stance, cartoon fantasy art style, vibrant colors.
generation.negative_prompt: high poly, realistic, cluttered, multiple creatures, base, pedestal, weapons, text

optimization.ktx2_note: toktx not installed on this machine; textures are WebP, not GPU-compressed KTX2. Re-run with KTX-Software installed for the Phase 6 KTX2 target.

assets.notes: Static base mesh. Raw FBX + PBR from Tripo, optimized to a game-ready GLB. Superseded as the player avatar by the rigged/animated variant below.

assets.source: tripo-starter
assets.license: Tripo Starter (paid) — verify commercial ownership terms before shipping

optimization.note: meshopt (not draco) used because the meshes are skinned/animated. simplify disabled to preserve rig weights.

assets.maps_to: player (temporary)
assets.forward_axis_note: This asset's forward is +X (Tripo rig convention), not -Z. Player.tsx corrects with faceOffset=-PI/2 so facing + stride align with movement. Any future clip/rig for this asset shares this offset.
assets.notes: Rigged biped + baked idle/run clips. Currently stands in as the player avatar (see AnimatedCharacter.tsx). Each clip is a separate GLB sharing one rig; the run clip retargets onto the idle mesh by bone name. Tripo's clip names both come through as 'NlaTrack' and are renamed idle/run in-app. Add slash/hurt/jump + a real state machine in Phase 2.

assets.notes: Static (unrigged) low-poly imp. Instanced per archetype in MonsterModels.tsx with faceOffset=-PI/2, displayHeight 1.4. No walk animation yet (monsters glide) — rig+animate later like the goblin.

assets.maps_to: player four-click melee combo
assets.notes: Every click is a complete damaging attack: horizontal, reverse horizontal, grounded overhead, and forward thrust. export_hero_combo.py preserves approved retargeted body mechanics and bakes the two custom IK deliveries onto the exact runtime hero rig.

assets.source: Original procedural Blender model created for GameDash
assets.license: Project-owned original asset
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit
assets.notes: Tall asymmetric dark-slate masonry wall, exactly 4m wide and 3m high with a bottom-center pivot. The 0.6m wall body has restrained integrated rubble extending the total depth to 0.792m. Uses a reusable seamless ruin-stone PBR set with base color, tangent-space normal, roughness breakup, mineral flecks, broken fissures, and sparse moss.

assets.source: Original GameDash geometry using the Poly Haven granite_wall photogrammetry material (https://polyhaven.com/a/granite_wall)
assets.license: Project-owned geometry; Poly Haven granite_wall material is CC0
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit

optimization.format: single-scene static GLB with Draco geometry and WebP textures

assets.notes: Production Low B wall is exactly 4m wide and 1.4m high with a bottom-center pivot. Revision v3 replaces the straight segmented slab construction with 31 individually warped stones across six uneven staggered courses. Stone heights, corner planes, depth positions, and rotations vary independently; missing upper stones form an asymmetric saddle crown, while seven angular rubble chunks extend total depth to 1.434m. The physically scaled 2K material spans 1.9m and supplies scanned base color, OpenGL normal, roughness, and AO data.

assets.source: Original GameDash geometry using the Poly Haven dark_rock scanned material (https://polyhaven.com/a/dark_rock)
assets.license: Project-owned geometry; Poly Haven dark_rock material is CC0
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit and outside-town portal landmarks

optimization.format: single-scene static GLB with Draco geometry and WebP textures

assets.notes: Massive gate-fragment v2 replaces the rejected small-brick arch. The exact 4m by 3.8m bottom-center module uses thick partially buried piers, large monolithic stones, a nearly continuous radial arch with one upper-right crown failure, broad displaced fracture planes, six boulder-scale rubble masses, and sparse moss ledges. Total depth is 2.224m and the central passage remains approximately 1.5m wide. The homogeneous 2.4m dark-rock scan contains no painted mortar joints, so geometry alone defines the architecture. Runtime collision should use two broad pier boxes while keeping the opening clear.

assets.source: Original GameDash geometry using the Poly Haven dark_rock scanned material (https://polyhaven.com/a/dark_rock)
assets.license: Project-owned geometry; Poly Haven dark_rock material is CC0
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit and outside-town vertical landmarks

optimization.format: single-scene static GLB with Draco geometry and WebP textures

assets.notes: Monumental intact column uses an exact 2m square bottom-center footprint and 4.6m height. A broad stepped plinth supports one continuous tapered octagonal shaft, architectural neck, flared capital, abacus, and oversized top slab. Weathering is limited to broad surface fractures, chipped non-load-bearing edges, and three restrained base rubble pieces, so the structure remains visibly intact. Vegetation is intentionally excluded and should be supplied by separate environment scatter assets.

assets.source: Original GameDash geometry using the Poly Haven dark_rock scanned material (https://polyhaven.com/a/dark_rock)
assets.license: Project-owned geometry; Poly Haven dark_rock material is CC0
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit and outside-town broken vertical landmarks

optimization.format: single-scene static GLB with Draco geometry and WebP textures

assets.notes: Broken companion column shares the intact column's exact 2m square bottom-center footprint and reaches 2.9m. The matching stepped plinth supports a continuous octagonal stump with a directional shear fracture: the highest rim survives back-left while the missing upper mass fell front-right. A horizontal side-readable shaft length, two capital fragments, and three subordinate rubble chips make the collapse legible without forming a uniform debris halo. Vegetation remains separate environment dressing.

assets.source: Original GameDash geometry using the Poly Haven dark_rock scanned material (https://polyhaven.com/a/dark_rock)
assets.license: Project-owned geometry; Poly Haven dark_rock material is CC0
assets.maps_to: modular ruined-town environment kit, path framing, and outside-town sightline breakup

optimization.format: single-scene static GLB with Draco geometry and WebP textures

assets.notes: Large landmark rubble uses an exact 4m by 3m bottom-center footprint and 1.55m height. Its 70/25/5 hierarchy combines three dominant collapsed masses with a fallen octagonal column length, capital slab, arch voussoir, four medium bridge pieces, and only four perimeter chips. Dominant components are sunk 0.10m and shallow undersides clipped to z=0 so the pile contacts the terrain instead of floating. The exported object origin is fixed at the true bottom-center (0,0,0). Collision should approximate only the three main masses and optional column segment.

assets.notes: Small rubble uses an exact 2m by 1.6m bottom-center footprint and 0.8m height. The compact 70/25/5 hierarchy combines two interlocked dominant forms with a fallen octagonal shaft fragment, three bridge pieces, and only three perimeter chips. Use one low dominant-mass collider and ignore the loose fragments.

assets.notes: Foundation slab uses an exact 4m square bottom-center footprint and 0.42m height. A buried continuous footing supports six broad fitted cap plates, a stable central support zone, exposed perimeter blocks, and one localized collapsed corner. Use one low 4m square collider and omit the corner rubble.

Intended for placement beyond the expedition play ring; keep collision disabled unless reused as an approachable landmark.

assets.notes: Grounded compression-arch v2: 11.67m-wide civic ruin with a roughly 4.68m clear opening, nine mutually bearing radial stones, oversized imposts, overlapping monolithic piers, independent buried plinths, rear buttresses, and a localized upper-right outer-wall collapse. The rejected floating v1 source is archived at assets/blender/archive/ruin_arch_distant_v1_floating.blend. It has no emissive material, portal VFX, or interaction language.
